metadata.title: 📖 kslearn User Guide
metadata.description: Complete user guide covering installation, setup, all features, tutorials, troubleshooting, and creating custom content. Learn how to use every part of kslearn.
metadata.author: kslearn

notes.title: What is kslearn?
notes.content:
kslearn is an offline-first educational learning system that combines comprehensive study notes, interactive quizzes, flashcards, step-by-step tutorials, and AI chat — all accessible from a single terminal interface.

Key Features:
• 📚 Study Notes — Detailed explanations with examples for every topic
• 📝 Interactive Quizzes — Multiple-choice questions with explanations
• 🃏 Flashcards — Quick review mode for key concepts
• 🎓 Tutorials — Step-by-step guided lessons
• 🤖 AI Chat — Online AI tutor (10+ providers) and offline mode
• 🧠 Knowledge Brain — Offline Q&A database that learns from you
• 📊 Progress Tracking — Scores, streaks, and daily goals
• 🏆 Achievements — 15 badges across 4 rarity levels
• 🔄 Spaced Review — Review topics you struggled with
• 🔍 Global Search — Search all notes at once

Everything works WITHOUT internet. Only AI Chat needs a connection (unless using Ollama locally or free providers like Sky and Phind).
key_points: kslearn is offline-first — all features work without internet
AI Chat is the only feature that may need internet (offline mode available)
All content is stored in JSON files for easy extension
Works on Termux, Linux, macOS, and Windows

examples.title: System Requirements
examples.explanation: What you need to run kslearn
examples.code:
Termux (Android): Python 3.7+, pkg install python
Linux: Python 3.7+, pip
macOS: Python 3.7+, pip
Windows: Python 3.7+, pip

Minimum storage: 50MB base installation
Recommended: 100MB+ for additional content packs

notes.title: Installation Guide
notes.content:
Installing kslearn is straightforward. Choose the method that matches your platform.

Quick Install (Recommended):
1. Clone the repository
2. Navigate to the kslearn directory
3. Install with pip

Termux Installation:
1. Install Python package manager
2. Clone the repository
3. Install kslearn

After installation, verify it worked by checking the version number.
key_points: Use 'pip install -e .' for development mode (editable install)
On Termux, run 'pkg install python' first
Verify with 'kslearn --version' — should show v1.0.0
The -e flag means changes to source code take effect immediately

examples.title: Installation Commands
examples.explanation: Copy-paste these commands to install
examples.code:
# Standard install
git clone https://github.com/kashsight/kslearn
cd kslearn
pip install -e .

# Termux
pkg install python
git clone https://github.com/kashsight/kslearn
cd kslearn
pip install -e .

# Verify
kslearn --version

notes.title: First Launch & Theme Setup
notes.content:
Run kslearn by typing 'kslearn' in your terminal. You'll see the main menu with the ASCII banner at the top.

Choosing a Theme:
kslearn has 3 standardized themes that change ALL UI colors — banner, tables, panels, menus, and prompts.

• Sky (sky_blue) — Default. Bright sky blue and cyan tones. Energizing.
• Forest (green) — Natural green theme. Calming for study sessions.
• Monochrome (grey) — Clean white and grey. Minimalist, distraction-free.

To change theme:
1. Press C from main menu for Settings
2. Select theme option
3. Choose green, grey, or sky_blue
4. Change applies immediately

Or edit data/config/settings.json:
"theme": "sky_blue"

Setting Your Username:
1. Press C for Settings
2. Select Username
3. Enter your name
This appears on progress reports and achievement notifications.
key_points: 3 themes available: sky_blue (default), green, grey
Themes change ALL UI colors consistently — banner, tables, menus, prompts
Theme set in settings.json under 'theme' key
Username appears on export reports and achievements

examples.title: Theme Color Mapping
examples.explanation: How each theme affects the UI
examples.code:
Sky Theme: banner=bright_cyan, primary=sky_blue1, border=sky_blue1
Green Theme: banner=bright_green, primary=green, border=green
Grey Theme: banner=white, primary=white, border=bright_black

All table columns, menu items, and prompts use the primary color.

notes.title: Main Menu Navigation
notes.content:
The main menu is your hub. Every feature is accessible with a single keypress.

Menu Options:
1 — Study Notes: Browse learning materials by category
2 — Take Quiz: Test your knowledge with multiple-choice questions
3 — AI Chat: Chat with AI tutor (online or offline)
4 — My Progress: View your statistics and scores
5 — Bookmarks: Access saved topics
6 — Knowledge Brain: Browse offline Q&A database
7 — Data Store: Download new content packs
8 — Support kslearn: Credits, email, website, GitHub
F — Flashcards: Quick card-based review
T — Timed Quiz: 60-second speed challenge
G — Global Search: Search all notes at once
R — Spaced Review: Review topics you struggled with
U — Tutorials: Step-by-step guided lessons
V — Achievements: View earned badges
E — Export Report: Generate progress summary as file
D — Profiles: Switch or manage user profiles
C — Settings: Configure themes, quiz options, AI
H or ? — Help: Show commands and usage info
0 — Exit: Leave kslearn

At the bottom of the menu you'll see:
• Daily Goal progress bar (minutes studied today)
• Study Streak (consecutive days studied)
• A tip suggesting to start with Study Notes
key_points: All 20 features accessible with one key from main menu
Numbered options (1-8) and letter shortcuts (F, T, G, R, U, V, E, D, C, H, 0)
Daily goal and streak shown at bottom of menu
Press Ctrl+C to cancel current operation or exit

examples.title: Quick Navigation Reference
examples.explanation: Memorize these for fast access
examples.code:
Study: 1 (notes), F (flashcards), U (tutorials)
Test: 2 (quiz), T (timed quiz)
AI: 3 (chat), 6 (knowledge brain)
Track: 4 (progress), V (achievements), R (spaced review)
Manage: 5 (bookmarks), D (profiles), C (settings)
Search: G (global search), E (export)
Help: H (?), 8 (support), 0 (exit)

notes.title: Study Notes — How to Learn
notes.content:
Study Notes is the core learning feature. Press 1 from the main menu.

Step 1: Select a Category
You'll see available content categories:
• 📖 Bible & Christianity — 25 topics covering the entire Bible
• 🏥 POTT Level 6 — 20 topics on Perioperative Theatre Technology
• 🧠 kslearn Platform — Platform guides and getting started

Step 2: Select a Topic
Each category lists its topics with numbers. Enter the number to study.

Step 3: Read the Content
Each topic displays:
• Full content — Detailed explanations with examples
• Key Points — Summary of important concepts
• Examples — Practical illustrations and code snippets

Step 4: Navigate
While reading:
• N — Next topic in the category
• P — Previous topic
• B — Back to topic list
• Q — Take a quiz on this topic
• Bookmark — Save topic for later review

Bible Content (25 topics):
Covers the entire Bible from Genesis to Revelation, including the Pentateuch, Historical Books, Prophets, Gospels, Epistles, parables, miracles, theology, end times, prayer, and practical topics like money, marriage, and suffering.

POTT Level 6 Content (20 topics):
TVET CDACC curriculum covering human anatomy, cell biology, homeostasis, hospital procedures, theatre safety, infection control, instruments, waste management, CPR, health education, diseases, medical ethics, and clinical services.
key_points: Select category first, then choose a specific topic
Each topic has content, key points, and examples
Use N/P to navigate between topics
Press Q to take a quiz on the current topic
Bookmark topics for quick access later (menu option 5)

examples.explanation: 25 topics from Genesis to practical Christian living
examples.code:
1. Introduction to the Bible
2. The Pentateuch (Torah)

notes.title: Quizzes — Testing Your Knowledge
notes.content:
Quizzes test your understanding with multiple-choice questions. Press 2 from the main menu.

Standard Quiz Mode:
1. Select a category (Bible, POTT, etc.)
2. Select a quiz topic
3. Answer each question by entering 1, 2, 3, or 4
4. After each answer you see:
   • ✓ (correct) or ✗ (wrong)
   • Explanation of why the answer is right
5. Final results show score, accuracy, and best streak

Standard quizzes have 6 questions per session.

Timed Quiz (press T from main menu):
• 60-second speed challenge
• Answer as many questions as possible
• Score based on correct answers in time limit
• Your best score is tracked
• Great for quick review sessions

Quiz Results Rating:
• 80%+ → 🎉 Excellent!
• 60-79% → 👍 Good job!
• Below 60% → 📚 Keep learning!

Quiz Features:
• Random topic selection available
• Review mode — retake only wrong answers
• Streak bonuses for consecutive correct answers
• Explanations shown after each answer (toggleable in settings)
• Scores saved per topic for progress tracking
key_points: Standard quiz = 6 questions with explanations
Timed quiz = 60 seconds, answer as many as possible
Results rated: 80%+ Excellent, 60-79% Good, <60% Keep learning
Explanations shown after each answer (can toggle in settings)
Scores saved automatically for progress tracking
Review mode lets you retake only wrong answers

examples.title: POTT Quiz Topics
examples.explanation: Available quiz topics in POTT Level 6
examples.code:
1. Human Anatomy & Physiology (10 questions)
   Covers: body organization, cells, homeostasis, skeletal system

2. Theatre Safety & Infection Control (10 questions)
   Covers: OT safety, nosocomial infections, sterilization

3. Disease Control & Health Education (10 questions)
   Covers: communicable diseases, health promotion, first aid

notes.title: Flashcards — Quick Review
notes.content:
Flashcards are the fastest way to review key concepts. Press F from the main menu.

How Flashcards Work:
1. Select a category (e.g., POTT Level 6)
2. A card appears showing the FRONT (question or concept)
3. Press Enter to FLIP and see the BACK (answer/explanation)
4. Rate your confidence:
   • E — Easy (you knew it immediately)
   • M — Medium (you needed to think)
   • H — Hard (you need to review again)
5. Next card appears automatically

Flashcard Features:
• Cards organized by difficulty: easy, medium, hard
• Progress tracked per session
• Score calculated from your ratings
• Great for quick review before exams

Current Flashcard Content:
• POTT Level 6: 25+ cards covering anatomy, procedures, safety
• kslearn Platform: 10 cards on platform features

Flashcard Tips:
• Be honest with your rating — hard cards come back sooner
• Use flashcards for 5-minute quick reviews
• Hard-rated cards help the spaced review system
key_points: Press Enter to flip between front and back of card
Rate: E (easy), M (medium), H (hard)
Hard ratings help spaced review identify weak topics
Current content: 25+ POTT cards, 10 platform cards
Great for 5-minute quick review sessions

examples.title: Sample Flashcard Flow
examples.explanation: How a flashcard session looks
examples.code:
FRONT: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
[Press Enter to flip]
BACK: Mitochondria — organelles that produce ATP energy

Rate your confidence:
E = Easy  M = Medium  H = Hard

Your choice: E
✓ Card marked as easy. Next card...

Session score: 8/10 easy (80%)

notes.title: Tutorials — Step-by-Step Lessons
notes.content:
Tutorials are structured, step-by-step guided lessons. Unlike notes (which you browse freely), tutorials present information in a fixed sequence.

Press U from the main menu to access tutorials.

Tutorial Structure:
Each tutorial has multiple steps:
1. Step title — What you'll learn in this step
2. Content — Detailed explanation
3. Example — Practical illustration
4. Tip — Important reminder or warning

Navigation:
• N — Next step
• P — Previous step
• Q — Take the tutorial's quiz
• B — Back to tutorial list

Current Tutorials (POTT Level 6):
1. Getting Started with POTT — Overview of the course and requirements
2. How to Sterilize Surgical Instruments — Proper sterilization methods
3. Managing OT Waste — Safe disposal of theatre waste
4. Performing CPR — Step-by-step CPR procedure
5. Understanding Theatre Zoning — OT zones and access control

Tutorial Features:
• Progress saved — resume where you left off
• Each tutorial ends with a quiz to test understanding
• Completion tracked in settings
• Achievement unlocked for completing your first tutorial (Tutorial Graduate)
key_points: Tutorials present content in a fixed step-by-step sequence
Progress is saved — you can resume later
Each tutorial has a quiz at the end
5 POTT tutorials currently available
Completing first tutorial earns 'Tutorial Graduate' achievement

examples.title: Tutorial: How to Sterilize Surgical Instruments
examples.explanation: Step breakdown of this tutorial
examples.code:
Step 1: Introduction to Sterilization
  — What sterilization is and why it matters
Step 2: Types of Sterilization Methods
  — Autoclave, dry heat, chemical sterilization
Step 3: Autoclave Operation
  — Temperature, pressure, time settings
Step 4: Quality Control
  — Biological and chemical indicators
Step 5: Storage of Sterile Instruments
  — Proper packaging and shelf life

Quiz: 5 questions on sterilization methods

notes.title: AI Chat — Your AI Tutor
notes.content:
AI Chat connects you with an AI tutor for personalized help. Press 3 from the main menu.

Two Modes:

1. ONLINE MODE — Uses tgpt with 10+ providers:

FREE Providers (no API key needed):
• Sky (sky-1 model)
• Phind (phind-70b model)
• DeepSeek (deepseek-chat)
• Pollinations AI
• ISou
• Kimi (free tier)

API Key Providers:
• OpenAI (gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo, gpt-3.5-turbo)
• Anthropic (claude-sonnet-4, claude-3-5-sonnet, claude-3-haiku)
• Groq (llama-3.2-90b, llama-3.1-70b, llama-3.1-8b)
• Google Gemini (gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.0-flash, gemini-1.5-pro)
• Ollama (llama3.3, llama3.2, mistral — runs locally)

2. OFFLINE MODE — Uses Knowledge Brain
• No internet needed
• Answers from built-in knowledge base
• Covers kslearn, DevOps, AI/ML, web development

Setting Up AI:
1. For free providers: Select Online → choose Sky or Phind → start chatting
2. For API providers: Settings → AI → Set API Key → paste key → select provider

AI Chat Features:
• Conversations auto-saved to Knowledge Brain
• Chat history keeps last 50 messages
• Category detection auto-tags saved Q&A
• Provider can be switched mid-session
• Auto-save can be toggled off in settings
key_points: Online mode needs internet; offline mode works without
Free providers (Sky, Phind, DeepSeek) need NO API key
All conversations auto-saved to Knowledge Brain
Chat history keeps last 50 messages
10+ providers available — switch anytime
Ollama runs locally — no internet needed

examples.title: Quick Start with Free AI
examples.explanation: No API key needed — just internet
examples.code:
1. Press 3 from main menu
2. Select 'Online'
3. Choose provider: Sky or Phind
4. Ask your question!

Example questions:
• 'Explain homeostasis'
• 'What happened in Acts chapter 2?'
• 'How do I sterilize surgical instruments?'
• 'What is the difference between mitosis and meiosis?'

notes.title: Knowledge Brain — Offline Q&A
notes.content:
The Knowledge Brain is a local Q&A database that works completely offline. Press 6 from the main menu.

How It Works:
All questions and answers are stored as JSON pairs. When you ask a question, the system searches stored knowledge for matching keywords.

Features:
• Search — Find answers by keyword
• Browse — Scroll through all stored knowledge
• Add — Manually add new Q&A pairs
• Export — Share knowledge as JSON file
• Import — Load knowledge from other users

How Knowledge Gets Added:
1. Automatically — AI Chat conversations are auto-saved
2. Manually — From the Knowledge Brain menu, select Add Q&A

Each Q&A pair contains:
• Question — The query
• Answer — The response
• Category — Topic area (devops, ai_ml, webdev, etc.)

Current Knowledge Covers:
• kslearn basics and features (20 pairs)
• DevOps & Cloud — Git, CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS
• AI/ML — Machine learning concepts
• Web Development — HTML, CSS, React, HTTP, DOM

Total: 34+ Q&A pairs across multiple categories
key_points: Works completely offline — no internet needed
AI Chat conversations auto-save to Knowledge Brain
You can manually add Q&A pairs
Knowledge can be exported and shared as JSON
34+ Q&A pairs currently stored
Search by keyword to find relevant answers

examples.title: Knowledge Brain Search
examples.explanation: Searching for stored knowledge
examples.code:
Search query: 'Docker'

Found 3 results:

1. What is Docker?
   Docker is a platform for containerizing applications.
   Category: devops_cloud

2. What is the difference between Docker and VMs?
   Docker uses containers (shared OS kernel) while VMs
   have full OS. Containers are lighter and faster.
   Category: devops_cloud

3. What is Docker Compose?
   Docker Compose defines and runs multi-container
   applications using a YAML configuration file.
   Category: devops_cloud

notes.title: Progress Tracking & Statistics
notes.content:
Track your learning journey with detailed statistics. Press 4 from the main menu.

Statistics Table Shows:
• Category — Subject area (Bible, POTT, etc.)
• Played — Number of quizzes taken
• Correct — Total correct answers
• Accuracy — Percentage score
• Best Streak — Longest consecutive correct answers

Study Streaks:
• Counts consecutive days you've studied
• Resets if you miss a day
• Best streak always tracked
• Streak bonuses give extra quiz points
• Achievements at 5, 10, and 30 days

Daily Goals:
• Set a daily study goal in minutes (default: 15)
• Progress bar shows minutes studied today
• Resets at midnight
• Achievement for reaching your goal
• Change in Settings → Daily Goal Minutes

All progress is saved automatically in data/config/settings.json including:
• Quiz scores per topic
• Accuracy percentages
• Completion timestamps
• Best streaks per category
• Bookmarks with timestamps
• Achievement earn dates
key_points: Statistics table shows quizzes, accuracy, and streaks per category
Study streak counts consecutive days — resets if you miss one
Daily goal defaults to 15 minutes — customizable in settings
All progress auto-saved to settings.json
Best streak and best scores are permanently tracked

examples.title: Statistics Table Example
examples.explanation: What your progress looks like

notes.title: Achievements & Badges
notes.content:
Earn achievements as you learn. Press V from the main menu to view your badges.

All 15 Achievements:

Common (easy to achieve):
🌟 First Steps — Complete your first quiz
🎓 Tutorial Graduate — Complete your first tutorial
🃏 Flash of Genius — Complete first flashcard session
👤 Profile Creator — Create a user profile

Uncommon (requires some effort):
💎 Perfectionist — Score 100% on any quiz
🏆 Quiz Master — Complete 25 quizzes
📖 Knowledge Seeker — Complete 50 Q&A questions
🗺️ Explorer — Study 5 different categories
📚 Bookworm — Bookmark 20 topics
🔄 Consistent Learner — Complete 10 spaced reviews

Rare (takes dedication):
🔥 Hot Streak — Maintain a 5-day study streak
⚡ Speed Demon — Score 15+ in timed quiz
🎨 Renaissance Mind — Score 80%+ in 3 categories

Legendary (only committed learners):
📅 Month Warrior — Maintain a 30-day study streak
🧠 Walking Encyclopedia — Complete 200 Q&A questions

Achievement notifications appear with the achievement name, description, and icon when unlocked.
key_points: 15 achievements across 4 rarity levels
Common: First Steps, Tutorial Graduate, Flash of Genius, Profile Creator
Uncommon: Perfectionist, Quiz Master, Knowledge Seeker, Explorer, Bookworm
Rare: Hot Streak (5 days), Speed Demon (15+ timed), Renaissance Mind
Legendary: Month Warrior (30 days), Walking Encyclopedia (200 Q&A)

examples.title: Achievement Unlocked Example
examples.explanation: What you see when earning an achievement

notes.title: Spaced Review System
notes.content:
Spaced repetition helps you review topics you struggled with, timing reviews just before you're likely to forget.

Press R from the main menu to access Spaced Review.

How It Works:
1. kslearn tracks topics where you scored below 80%
2. These topics are added to your Review Queue
3. Each topic has a next review date
4. Review the topic and retake the quiz
5. If you improve, next review is pushed further out

Review Schedule (based on forgetting curve):
• First review: 1 day after struggling
• Second review: 3 days after
• Third review: 7 days after
• Fourth review: 14 days after

This system maximizes retention by reviewing just before you forget.

Review Queue Tracks:
• Category and topic name
• Your accuracy score
• Last reviewed date
• Review count
• Next review date

Tips:
• Don't skip spaced reviews — they target your weak areas
• Hard-rated flashcards also feed the review queue
• Completing 10 spaced reviews earns the 'Consistent Learner' achievement
key_points: Spaced review targets topics where you scored below 80%
Review schedule: 1 day, 3 days, 7 days, 14 days
Based on the forgetting curve for maximum retention
Hard flashcard ratings feed the review queue
10 spaced reviews earns 'Consistent Learner' achievement

examples.title: Review Queue Example
examples.explanation: What your review queue looks like
examples.code:
📅 Spaced Review Queue

1. Cell Structure and the Cell Cycle
   Accuracy: 40% | Next review: Tomorrow

2. Homeostasis and Feedback Loops
   Accuracy: 60% | Next review: In 2 days

3. Paul's Epistles
   Accuracy: 70% | Next review: In 5 days

Total items in queue: 3
Press R to start reviewing!

notes.title: Global Search
notes.content:
Search ALL notes across ALL categories at once. Press G from the main menu.

How It Works:
1. Enter a search term (e.g., 'cell', 'salvation', 'CPR')
2. kslearn searches all categories and topics
3. Results show matching topics with highlighted keywords
4. Select a result to read the full topic

Search Features:
• Case-insensitive — 'cell' finds 'Cell', 'CELL'
• Searches content, key points, and examples
• Partial matches work — 'homeo' finds 'homeostasis'
• Cross-category — one search covers Bible, POTT, and all content
• Results ranked by relevance

Search Tips:
• Use specific terms for better results ('mitochondria' vs 'cell')
• Search for concepts, not just keywords
• Try related terms if first search returns nothing
• Search works across all installed content packs
key_points: Searches ALL categories at once
Case-insensitive and supports partial matches
Searches content, key points, and examples
Results show matching topics with highlighted keywords
Works across all installed content packs

examples.title: Search Results Example
examples.explanation: What search results look like
examples.code:
🔍 Search: 'mitochondria'

Found 3 matches:

1. 🏥 POTT Level 6 → Cell Structure and the Cell Cycle
   ...contains: Mitochondrion — powerhouse of the cell...

2. 📖 Bible → Organization of the Human Body
   ...contains: cells containing organelles such as
   the nucleus, mitochondria, and ribosomes...

3. 🧠 Knowledge Brain → What is the powerhouse of the cell?
   ...contains: Mitochondria — organelles that produce ATP

Select a result number to read full topic (0 to exit):

notes.title: Data Store — Download Content
notes.content:
The Data Store lets you browse and download new learning content. Press 7 from the main menu.

Free Content Packs:
• Python Basics Pack
• JavaScript Essentials
• AI & Machine Learning Intro
• Cybersecurity Fundamentals
• Web Development Bootcamp

Premium Content:
• Complete Python Mastery ($9.99)
• Full-Stack Web Developer Path ($19.99)
• Machine Learning Engineer ($24.99)
• Cybersecurity Professional ($14.99)
• Data Science Bootcamp ($17.99)
• DevOps Engineer Path ($16.99)

Community Content:
• Rust Programming Basics
• Go for Backend

Bundles:
• All-Access Pass ($49.99 one-time)
• Monthly Subscription ($9.99/month)

How to Download:
1. Select a content pack
2. Choose Download
3. Pack downloads as .ksl file (encrypted, signed)
4. kslearn auto-installs the content
5. New categories appear in Study Notes and Quizzes

Note: Free content needs internet for download. Premium content requires purchase.
key_points: 5 free content packs available
6 premium content packs ($9.99-$24.99 each)
2 community content packs (Rust, Go)
Bundles: All-Access ($49.99) or Monthly ($9.99/month)
Content downloads as .ksl encrypted files
Auto-installs after download

examples.title: Content Pack Contents
examples.explanation: What's inside each pack
examples.code:
Python Basics Pack (Free):
  • Notes: Python syntax, variables, data types, operators
  • Quiz: 10 questions on Python fundamentals
  • Flashcards: 15 cards on Python concepts

Complete Python Mastery (Premium, $9.99):
  • Notes: 30+ topics from basics to advanced
  • Quiz: 50+ questions across 5 quiz sets
  • Flashcards: 40 cards
  • Tutorials: 8 step-by-step tutorials

notes.title: Settings Reference
notes.content:
Configure your kslearn experience. Press C from the main menu.

Theme Settings:
• Theme — green, grey, sky_blue (default: sky_blue)
• Color Scheme — Matches selected theme

Quiz Settings:
• Difficulty — easy, medium, hard, mixed (default: mixed)
• Show Explanations — Show answer explanations (default: true)
• Auto Timer — Enable timed quizzes (default: true)
• Timer Minutes — Quiz duration (default: 3)

AI Settings:
• API Provider — OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, Gemini, Ollama (default: anthropic)
• API Key — Your provider's API key (default: empty)
• Auto-Save Chat — Save AI chat to Knowledge Brain (default: true)

General Settings:
• Username — Your display name (default: empty)
• Language — Interface language (default: English)
• Daily Goal — Minutes per day (default: 15)
• Focus Track — Default learning track (default: general)
• Notifications — Enable notifications (default: true)
• Sound — Enable sound effects (default: false)
• Use Rich UI — Enable Rich terminal UI (default: true)

Feature Toggles (all default: true):
• Flashcards
• Timed Quiz
• Global Search
• Spaced Review
• Tutorials
• Achievements
• Export Report
• Multi-User Profiles
• Study Streaks
• Daily Goal Tracking
key_points: Theme: sky_blue (default), green, grey
Quiz difficulty: mixed (default) — or set to easy/medium/hard
API Provider: anthropic (default) — change to any supported provider
Daily goal: 15 minutes (default) — adjust to your schedule
All features enabled by default — toggle off if not needed
Settings saved to data/config/settings.json

examples.title: Settings JSON Structure
examples.explanation: How settings are stored
examples.code:
{
  "theme": "sky_blue",
  "color_scheme": "sky_blue",
  "username": "gombwe",

  "daily_goal_minutes": 15,
  "api_provider": "anthropic",
  "use_rich_ui": true,
  "flashcard_mode": true,
  "features": {
    "flashcards": true,
    "timed_quiz": true,
    "global_search": true,
    "spaced_review": true,
    "tutorials": true,
    "achievements": true
  }
}

notes.title: Export Report
notes.content:
Generate a comprehensive progress summary. Press E from the main menu.

What the Report Includes:
• All quiz scores and accuracy per category
• Study streak (current and best)
• Achievements earned with dates
• Topics bookmarked
• Tutorials completed
• Total questions answered
• Daily goal completion history

Export Formats:
• Markdown (.md) — Default, readable in any text editor
• Text (.txt) — Plain text format

How to Export:
1. Press E from main menu
2. Enter a filename (or accept default: kslearn_report.md)
3. File saves to your current directory

Use Cases:
• Share with teachers or parents
• Keep for personal records
• Include in learning portfolios
• Track progress over weeks/months
• Compare performance across categories
key_points: Report includes scores, streaks, achievements, bookmarks

Default format: Markdown (.md)
• Also supports plain text (.txt)
Default filename: kslearn_report.md
Useful for sharing with teachers or keeping records

examples.title: Sample Report Output
examples.explanation: What an exported report looks like

notes.title: User Profiles
notes.content:
Multiple users can share the same kslearn installation with separate progress. Press D from the main menu.

Why Use Profiles:
• Multiple users on one device
• Separate progress for different purposes (school, personal, work)
• Each profile has its own settings

Each Profile Tracks Separately:
• Quiz history and scores
• Achievements earned
• Bookmarked topics
• Study streaks
• Tutorial progress
• Daily goals
• Review queue
• Flashcard sessions

To Create a Profile:
1. Press D from main menu
2. Select Create Profile
3. Enter a profile name
4. Profile created with clean slate

To Switch Profiles:
1. Press D
2. Select the profile name
3. All data switches to that profile

Default Profile:
Every installation comes with a 'default' profile. Your current progress is stored under this profile.
key_points: Each profile has separate quiz history, achievements, streaks, bookmarks
Create unlimited profiles
Default profile created automatically
Switch profiles instantly from main menu
Useful for shared devices or separate learning tracks

examples.title: Profile Structure
examples.explanation: How profiles are stored in settings

notes.title: Creating Custom Content — JSON Format
notes.content:
You can create your own learning content as JSON files. Place them in the data/ directory and kslearn loads them automatically.

A content file has this structure:

1. METADATA — Describes the content package:
   • category: Unique identifier (lowercase, underscores)
   • title: Display name with emoji
   • description: Brief summary
   • icon: Emoji for the category
   • difficulty: beginner, intermediate, advanced
   • version: Content version number
   • author: Creator name

2. NOTES — Array of study topics:
   Each note has: id, title, icon, content, key_points[], examples[]

3. QUIZ_TOPICS — Array of quiz question sets:
   Each has: topic name, questions array
   Each question has: id, question, options[], answer, explanation

4. FLASHCARDS — Array of front/back cards:
   Each has: front, back, category, difficulty

5. TUTORIALS — Array of step-by-step lessons:
   Each has: title, steps[]
   Each step has: title, content, example, tip

You can include any combination — notes only, quizzes only, or all four types.
key_points: Place JSON files in data/ directory — kslearn auto-loads them
Metadata block is required; notes, quizzes, flashcards, tutorials are optional
Category should be unique (lowercase with underscores)
Quiz answer is zero-based index (0=first option, 1=second)
You can include any combination of content types

examples.title: Complete Content File Template
examples.explanation: Full JSON structure for a content file

notes.title: Updating and Troubleshooting
notes.content:
Keeping kslearn up to date and solving common problems.

Updating kslearn:
1. Navigate to kslearn directory
2. Run: git pull
3. Run: pip install -e .

Backing Up Progress:
Your progress is in data/config/settings.json
Backup: cp data/config/settings.json settings_backup.json
Restore: cp settings_backup.json data/config/settings.json

Common Issues:

Problem: 'kslearn: command not found'
Solution: Run 'pip install -e .' from the kslearn directory

Problem: No content in Study Notes
Solution: Check that JSON files exist in the data/ directory

Problem: AI Chat not working
Solution: Check internet connection. For free providers (Sky, Phind), no API key needed. For offline mode, check data/brain/knowledge.json exists.

Problem: Quiz won't start
Solution: Ensure the content file has quiz_topics with questions

Problem: Progress not saving
Solution: Check write permissions on data/config/settings.json

Problem: Colors look wrong
Solution: Change theme in Settings (C) to sky_blue, green, or grey

Getting Help:
• Press H in main menu for built-in help
• Visit: https://github.com/kashsight/kslearn
• Use option 8 (Support) for contact info
key_points: Update with 'git pull' then 'pip install -e .'
Backup settings.json to preserve progress
Most issues fixed by reinstalling with 'pip install -e .'
AI Chat needs internet except for offline/Knowledge Brain mode
Progress stored in data/config/settings.json
Theme issues fixed by switching to sky_blue, green, or grey

examples.title: Quick Fix Commands
examples.explanation: Common troubleshooting commands
examples.code:
# Reinstall (fixes most issues)
pip install -e .

# Backup progress
cp data/config/settings.json settings_backup_$(date +%F).json

# Restore progress
cp settings_backup.json data/config/settings.json

# Check Python version
python --version

# Check installed packages
pip list | grep kslearn

# Check content files
ls data/*.json
ls data/brain/*.json

questions.question: What command installs kslearn in development mode?
questions.explanation: pip install -e . installs in editable/development mode, so source code changes take effect immediately.

questions.question: Which feature in kslearn requires internet?
questions.explanation: Only AI Chat online mode needs internet. Everything else works offline. AI Chat also has an offline mode using Knowledge Brain.

questions.question: How many themes does kslearn have?
questions.explanation: kslearn has 3 themes: Sky (sky_blue), Forest (green), and Monochrome (grey).

questions.question: Where is user progress stored?
questions.explanation: All user progress — quiz scores, streaks, achievements, bookmarks — is stored in data/config/settings.json.

questions.question: What key opens the main menu's Help screen?
questions.explanation: Press H or ? from the main menu to see the help screen with all commands and usage info.

questions.question: What does the Spaced Review feature do?
options: Reviews topics you scored below 80% on a schedule
Repeats the same quiz every day
questions.explanation: Spaced Review tracks topics where you scored below 80% and schedules reviews at increasing intervals (1, 3, 7, 14 days) based on the forgetting curve.

questions.question: How many achievements are there in kslearn?
questions.explanation: kslearn has 15 achievements across 4 rarity levels: Common (4), Uncommon (6), Rare (3), and Legendary (2).

questions.question: Which AI provider runs locally without internet?
questions.explanation: Ollama runs AI models locally on your device, so it doesn't need internet. All other providers require a connection.

questions.question: What file format does the Data Store use for content packs?
questions.explanation: Content packs download as .ksl files, which are encrypted and signed for security.

questions.question: What accuracy rating earns 'Excellent!' in quiz results?
questions.explanation: 80%+ accuracy earns 'Excellent!' (🎉). 60-79% is 'Good job!' (👍). Below 60% is 'Keep learning!' (📚).

questions.question: Where should you place custom JSON content files?
questions.explanation: Custom JSON content files go in the data/ directory. kslearn auto-discovers and loads them.

questions.question: In quiz questions, what does 'answer': 2 mean?
questions.explanation: The answer field uses zero-based indexing. answer: 0 = first option, answer: 1 = second, answer: 2 = third option.

questions.question: Which field is REQUIRED in a content file's metadata?
questions.explanation: The minimum required metadata fields are category (unique identifier), title (display name), and description (summary). Other fields are recommended but not strictly required.

questions.question: How do you create line breaks in note content?
questions.explanation: Use \n (escaped newline) in JSON strings to create line breaks in note content.

questions.question: Can a content file include only flashcards without notes?
options: Yes, any combination of content types works
Only if the metadata says flashcard_only
questions.explanation: You can include any combination — notes only, quizzes only, flashcards only, tutorials only, or any mix.

flashcards.back:
pip install -e .

The -e flag installs in editable mode so source changes take effect immediately.

Sky (sky_blue), Forest (green), Monochrome (grey)

Default is Sky. Change in Settings (C).

flashcards.front: Which key opens Flashcards from the main menu?
flashcards.back:
F

F = Flashcards, T = Timed Quiz, G = Global Search, R = Spaced Review, U = Tutorials, V = Achievements, E = Export, D = Profiles, C = Settings, H = Help, 0 = Exit

flashcards.front: How many achievements does kslearn have?
flashcards.back:
15 achievements across 4 rarity levels:
• Common: 4
• Uncommon: 6
• Rare: 3
• Legendary: 2

flashcards.front: What accuracy earns 'Excellent!' in a quiz?
flashcards.back:
80%+ accuracy

60-79% = Good job!
Below 60% = Keep learning!

flashcards.front: What is the Spaced Review schedule?
flashcards.back:
Based on the forgetting curve:
1. First review: 1 day after
2. Second review: 3 days after
3. Third review: 7 days after
4. Fourth review: 14 days after

flashcards.front: Which AI providers are FREE (no API key)?
flashcards.back:
Sky, Phind, DeepSeek, Pollinations AI, ISou, Kimi (free tier)

Ollama runs locally.
OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, Gemini need API keys.

flashcards.front: What file format do Data Store content packs use?
flashcards.back:
.ksl files — encrypted and signed content packages.

They auto-install after download.

flashcards.front: In quiz JSON, what does 'answer': 1 mean?
flashcards.back:
The SECOND option is correct (zero-based index).

answer: 0 = first option
answer: 1 = second option
answer: 2 = third option
answer: 3 = fourth option

flashcards.front: How many topics are in the Bible content?
flashcards.back: 25 topics — covering the entire Bible from Genesis to Revelation, including theology, parables, miracles, prayer, and practical topics.

1. Getting Started with POTT
2. How to Sterilize Surgical Instruments
3. Managing OT Waste
4. Performing CPR
5. Understanding Theatre Zoning

flashcards.front: How do you create a new user profile?
flashcards.back:
Press D from main menu → Select Create Profile → Enter name.

Each profile has separate progress, achievements, streaks, and settings.

ALL notes across ALL categories at once.

It searches content, key points, and examples. Case-insensitive with partial match support.

cd kslearn && git pull && pip install -e .

Then backup your settings.json before updating.

steps.title: Step 1: Install kslearn
steps.content:
kslearn is a terminal-based learning system. You need Python installed first.

On Termux: pkg install python
Then clone and install: git clone + pip install -e .
steps.example:
git clone https://github.com/kashsight/kslearn
cd kslearn
pip install -e .
kslearn --version
steps.tip: The -e flag means any changes you make to the source code take effect immediately without reinstalling.

steps.title: Step 2: Launch kslearn
steps.content:
Simply type 'kslearn' in your terminal. You'll see the main menu with the ASCII art banner and all available features.

The menu shows 20 features with both numbered options (1-8) and letter shortcuts (F, T, G, R, U, V, E, D, C, H, 0).
steps.example:
kslearn
# You'll see the banner, menu table, daily goal, and study streak
steps.tip: Press H at any time from the main menu to see a help screen with all commands.

steps.title: Step 3: Choose Your Theme
steps.content:
kslearn has 3 themes. Press C for Settings, then select the theme option. Choose from sky_blue (default), green, or grey.

The theme changes ALL colors in the UI — the banner, menu tables, panels, prompts, and borders all use one consistent color palette.
steps.example:
Sky: bright_cyan banner, sky_blue1 tables
Green: bright_green banner, green tables
Grey: white banner, bright_black borders
steps.tip: Try all three themes and pick the one that's most comfortable for your eyes during long study sessions.

steps.title: Step 4: Start Studying
steps.content:
Press 1 to open Study Notes. Select a category, then pick a topic. Read through the content, key points, and examples.

After studying, press 2 to take a quiz on what you learned. Or press T for a quick timed quiz.
steps.example: 1 → Select category → Select topic → Read → N (next) → Q (quiz)
steps.tip: The recommended learning flow is: Study Notes → Quiz → Flashcards for review → Come back tomorrow for your streak!

steps.title: Step 5: Track Your Progress
steps.content:
Press 4 to see your statistics table. Check your accuracy per category, best streaks, and study streak.

Set a daily goal in settings (default 15 minutes). The progress bar at the bottom of the main menu shows how close you are.
steps.example: 4 → View stats → C → Settings → Daily Goal → Set to 30 minutes
steps.tip: Study a little every day to build your streak. 5 days = Hot Streak achievement, 30 days = Month Warrior (legendary)!

tutorials.title: How to Create Your Own Content

steps.title: Step 1: Create the JSON File
steps.content:
Create a new .json file in the data/ directory. Name it something descriptive like my_subject.json.

Start with the metadata block — this tells kslearn about your content.
steps.tip: Use a unique category name — lowercase with underscores. This is how kslearn identifies your content.

steps.title: Step 2: Add Study Notes
steps.content:
Add a 'notes' array to your JSON file. Each note needs an id, title, icon, content, key_points array, and examples array.

Use \n for line breaks in the content field.
steps.tip: Write 3-6 key points per topic. Keep topics focused — one concept per topic works best.

steps.title: Step 3: Add Quiz Questions
steps.content:
Add a 'quiz_topics' array. Each quiz topic has a name and an array of questions.

Each question needs: id, question text, 4 options, answer index, and explanation.

IMPORTANT: answer is zero-based — 0 means first option, 1 means second, etc.
steps.tip: Write plausible wrong options. Make questions test understanding, not just memorization. Always include a helpful explanation.

steps.title: Step 4: Add Flashcards and Tutorials (Optional)
steps.content:
Add flashcards for quick review — each has a front (question) and back (answer).

Add tutorials for step-by-step guided lessons — each tutorial has a title and an array of steps.
steps.tip: Flashcards are great for definitions and key facts. Tutorials work best for procedures and workflows.

steps.title: Step 5: Test Your Content
steps.content:
Save the JSON file and run 'kslearn'. Your new category should appear in Study Notes.

Test by:
1. Reading a note (press 1)
2. Taking the quiz (press 2)
3. Trying flashcards (press F)
4. Running the tutorial (press U)

If anything doesn't work, check your JSON syntax.
steps.example:
# Validate JSON syntax:
python -m json.tool data/my_subject.json

# Then launch:
kslearn
steps.tip: Use 'python -m json.tool' to validate your JSON before launching. It catches syntax errors like missing commas or brackets.
